symptoms.symptom_name: Lightheadedness and darkening of vision immediately on standing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Presyncope precisely time-locked to standing is the cardinal feature of orthostatic hypotension.
Presyncopal blacking-out is not rotatory vertigo.

symptoms.symptom_name: Relief of symptoms within seconds of sitting or lying down

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid resolution with recumbency reflects restoration of venous return and cerebral perfusion — characteristic of orthostatic hypotension.
Arrhythmic presyncope is not reliably abolished by posture change alone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Near-syncope with collapse back onto the bed on standing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Near-collapse triggered by standing indicates a significant postural drop in cerebral perfusion.
Any near-syncope in an older adult obliges exclusion of an arrhythmic/ischaemic cause before a benign label.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent up-titration of alpha-blocker (tamsulosin) for BPH

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Alpha-1 blockade impairs reflex vasoconstriction on standing; a recent dose increase is a strong iatrogenic precipitant.

symptoms.symptom_name: Coat-hanger ache across the neck and shoulders when upright

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sub-occipital/shoulder 'coat-hanger' ache from postural muscle ischaemia is relatively specific for orthostatic hypotension.

symptoms.symptom_name: No palpitations; no symptoms while seated at rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of palpitations and of at-rest events lowers, but does not eliminate, the probability of an arrhythmic cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: No true rotatory vertigo (blacking-out, not spinning)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of rotatory vertigo makes a vestibular cause unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Postural blood pressure fall >30/15 mmHg on active stand testing

investigation_details.investigation_name: Active stand (orthostatic) blood pressure and heart rate measurement
investigation_details.expected_result: Supine 135/80 mmHg (HR 72) falling to 105/65 mmHg (HR 88) at 3 minutes standing — a sustained >30/15 mmHg drop
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without measured orthostatic vitals the diagnosis cannot be confirmed and its severity cannot be graded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sustained postural fall of >=20/10 mmHg within 3 minutes defines orthostatic hypotension; this clinician-measured sign is sealed and not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular cardiac rhythm, no murmurs, normal-volume peripheral pulses supine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A regular rhythm on examination lowers the likelihood of an arrhythmic cause but does not exclude an intermittent one — clinician-measured, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal neurological deficit and no signs of head trauma

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal neurological examination and absence of injury reassure but do not point to a specific diagnosis — clinician-measured, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG to exclude arrhythmia or ischaemia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An ECG is required to exclude an arrhythmic/ischaemic cause of presyncope and is not obtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Reproducible presyncope on standing, rapid relief on recumbency, and coat-hanger ache together strongly indicate orthostatic hypotension.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Active stand (orthostatic) blood pressure and heart rate measurement
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and grades orthostatic hypotension; must be performed in person as it cannot be done via chat.
Excludes an arrhythmic or ischaemic cause of presyncope in an elderly patient before a benign diagnosis is accepted.
Identifies the recent tamsulosin up-titration as the likely iatrogenic precipitant and guides withholding/adjustment.
No head strike and no focal neurology — imaging is not indicated for this non-traumatic near-fall and would be over-investigation.
